title: John Coltrane
extract: John William Coltrane was an American jazz saxophonist, bandleader, and composer. His 1960 album Giant Steps contained only his own compositions; its title track is generally considered to have one of the most difficult chord progressions of any widely played jazz composition. Jazz critic Ira Gitler coined the term "sheets of sound" to describe the dense, rapid style Coltrane developed while playing with Thelonious Monk. Over the course of his career his music took on an increasingly spiritual dimension, exemplified on his most acclaimed album, A Love Supreme (1965). He died in 1967, and was awarded a special Pulitzer Prize in 2007 citing his "masterful improvisation, supreme musicianship and iconic centrality to the history of jazz."
